title: galium mollugo subsp. album
intro: Galium mollugo subsp. album, also known as White Bedstraw, is a subspecies of the Galium mollugo plant, which belongs to the family Rubiaceae. This perennial herb is native to Europe and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The health benefits of White Bedstraw include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. White Bedstraw can be prepared as a herbal tea, infused oil, or tincture, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed and utilized in a variety of ways.
